title: Production wiring missing: subscribeEditingSessionState() is never called and createBlockEditorAdapter() is never instantiated outside of tests
description:
REQ-FEED-029 mandates that '+page.svelte または FeedList.svelte が subscribeEditingSessionState(...) を 1 回 mount し, unmount 時に unsubscribe する'. REQ-FEED-030 mandates that 'BlockEditorAdapter の生成は +page.svelte または FeedList で 1 回行い、FeedRow に props として渡す'. Verification architecture §14 (line 757) explicitly assigns this responsibility to +page.svelte. None of these calls exist in production code.

+page.svelte (lines 1-132) imports neither editingSessionChannel nor createBlockEditorAdapter; it never calls subscribeEditingSessionState; it does not pass editingSessionState or blockEditorAdapter to FeedList.

FeedList.svelte (lines 1-50, 373-391) does not declare editingSessionState or blockEditorAdapter as Props; it never calls subscribeEditingSessionState; the <FeedRow ...> mount block does not forward those props.

Consequence: in production, FeedRow.svelte ALWAYS receives editingSessionState=null and blockEditorAdapter=null (their default values). The mount predicate `shouldMountBlocks && blockEditorAdapter` (FeedRow.svelte line 448) is therefore ALWAYS false. No BlockElement will ever render in the running application, no fallback dispatch chain will ever fire, and the entire Sprint 5 in-place editing surface is non-functional. The user 'click past note → empty paragraph appears → can type' scenario described in REQ-FEED-031 cannot occur.

This is a complete failure to deliver the user-facing behavior the sprint promised.

additionalEvidence: promptnotes/src/lib/feed/FeedList.svelte:373-391 (FeedRow mount block does not forward editingSessionState or blockEditorAdapter)
promptnotes/src/lib/feed/FeedRow.svelte:448 (mount predicate `{#if shouldMountBlocks && blockEditorAdapter}` requires non-null adapter)

expectedBehavior: +page.svelte (or FeedList.svelte) calls `const adapter = createBlockEditorAdapter()` once at module init or in $effect, calls `subscribeEditingSessionState(handler)` once in $effect with cleanup, holds the latest payload in $state, and threads both values down to FeedList → FeedRow as props.
actualBehavior: Both wiring sites are absent. Production FeedRow always sees editingSessionState=null and blockEditorAdapter=null. Block editing surface never mounts.
